team_news: Netherlands are built around Virgil van Dijk, Frenkie de Jong and Cody Gakpo/Depay attacking routes, but multiple previews flag injury losses or doubts including Jurrien Timber and Xavi Simons.
Japan are described as fast, technical and high-pressing, with Takefusa Kubo, Wataru Endo, Takehiro Tomiyasu and Zion Suzuki among the core names.
Japan's recent form indicators and historical wins over elite sides are why the away/draw protection is preferred over a pure upset pick.
weather_venue: Dallas Stadium is a retractable-roof NFL venue; exact match climate/weather status was not confirmed.
General World Cup weather coverage flags heat, humidity and storms as tournament-wide risks, but indoor conditions should mute direct impact if closed.
historical_structural: Netherlands are three-time World Cup finalists and the stronger historical football nation.
Japan have become a consistent World Cup knockout-level side and are no longer a simple underdog profile.
First group match with both teams likely treating one point as useful, raising draw probability.

risks: Sporttery odds, handicap line and single-match status unavailable.
